title: My Video about Making Videos - Request from Cell Message

transcript.text: Somebody sent me a text message telling me to do a video about making videos.
So, okay, here's my video about making videos.
I guess I'll just talk about how I make videos.
Okay, well, like my talking videos.
How do I make my talking videos?
I basically make a talking video when I can remember that there was something that I wanted to talk about.
But throughout the day, every once in a while, you kind of get an idea and then you think about something you want to talk about.
And then, like, you forget that that was the video that you wanted to do something that you wanted to talk about.
But sometimes you do remember again.
And so, like, and then sometimes I feel like I have time to make a video.
Like, you know, like I don't feel totally, like, crunched.
Like, oh, my God, I have to get to the library right now to work on my thing.
Or, you know, I'm not, like, sometimes I just don't feel good.
Like, maybe I'm sick and I'm just tired and I want to go to bed or something.
Or it's dark out, you know, and there's not good lighting.
I don't like to make videos when it's dark anymore because I like to be able to see the face.
And, yeah, so, like, whenever I have time to make a video, and I don't have time to make videos a lot.
I know that sounds kind of lame because it doesn't take a long time to make videos.
But I'm, like, really, like, addicted like a crackhead to my timeline project.
I just don't have the time for it.
I mean, I don't have the time to exercise.
I don't even exercise at all.
You know, I never take showers.
I don't hang out with people.
I don't do anything.
But also, I guess there's a factor of, yeah, you kind of have to be in the mood.
But, like, that's why I'm up here in the mountains.
I'm going to be up here in the mountains for a week, and I'm just going to be hanging out in my car all day every day making videos.
I'm going to ride my bike up to the top of the lookout and do a panorama video on one day.
And I'll probably do some stretching and maybe some weightlifting if it's warm.
It's been kind of cold.
Yesterday it was nice.
But, yeah, like, I basically don't have time.
So I'm up here for a week, and I'm just making videos, making videos.
And another thing is, like, I can't always make videos all the time because I can't always upload them.
Uploading them is a big issue for me.
Usually, like, if I'm in a gym and I make, like, a video that's, like, a half hour of talking, it would take, like, three hours to upload.
And a lot of times I would have to upload it, like, four or five times.
And I don't like to make new videos before I've uploaded a video because I'm just really paranoid that I might lose that video.
I lose videos all the time.
Like, shit just disappears.
I don't know what the hell happens.
I must be really loopy.
But I don't, yeah, I don't want to, like, make, spend all the time talking about making a video and then it disappears.
Like, once I make it, I need to upload it right away and forget about it.
And that's that, you know?
I'm not, like, upload videos and then I can't upload it.
So then I frickin' record more videos.
Yeah, so, but I don't think about, I don't, like, write notes and think about stuff that I say in the video.
I just think about it.
You know, I remember I was going to talk about something and then I make the video and I talk about it.
Because for me, I'm a quality, quantity before quality guy.
Like, I don't, like, comb my hair and shave and, like, make sure that I'm totally awake and stuff and remember to do my inhaler.
You know, and, like, take notes and think about for a few minutes every time I make a video.
Because it adds time.
I don't have time for that shit.
And, you know, the amount of videos I have on YouTube is a direct relation, is directly related to how much spare time I have.
And I would, I elect to have more videos and to have longer videos than to have less videos that are shorter, you know?
And I think it's a cool thing to do because, like, if I think about people on YouTube who I like, who I think is really smart and they talk about stuff,

I would prefer them to have a half hour long video where they're looking, where their hair isn't combed and they're a little bit, like, not totally with it mentally because they're tired or something.
But they're saying more because it's a half hour than if it was just, like, five minutes of them, like, totally on top of it.
Like, yeah, and there you go.
You know, or I'd rather have somebody, like, with 2,000 videos up than somebody with, like, 50.
You know, if I like what they talk about.
And also I like to hear what they talk about other stuff that's not related to the stuff that they're talking about.
I like to hear people talk about a wide range of things because it helps put their original thing that they were talking about in perspective.

Like, if somebody is a really good history teacher and they're talking about the history of this, history of that, and then they get up in the modern times and they start saying some stuff about history, but it kind of seems like they're a little bit anti-New Age.
I'd like to know what their stances are on all the, like, political issues and New Age stances.
And if they turn out to be a little bit like neocon, then I can listen to their history lecture again and I can see it through a neocon perspective and I can learn more about it that way.
And I can actually learn more.
So it's good.
It's better to do it that way.
And so that's why my philosophy of just quantity before quality is there.
And so that's how I make videos.
Because I don't, and I don't think before I do the video, I just do it.
From now, I try to do all of my videos in my car because the acoustics are really good in here.
It's just the perfect size to reverberate the sound.
The table here that I'm using, and I put the camera on top of it, it's perfectly close to my face.
You can see everything.
It's below my face.
You know, like, there's level.
And I look better at this angle than I do this angle.
You know, like, even my mom complained.
She's like, the videos, you're looking up to you and it makes it seem like you think you're better than everybody.
And I'm like, and it's ugly and I don't like it.
And it's like, yeah, but I don't have, like, a bunch of books to put my tripod on.
If I did, I should do that, actually.
I think from now on, I am going to do that.
That's a good idea.
You know what?
I'm going to start that on this video right now.
Well, I'm going to put my thingy on my computer.
And then I'm going to put my computer on top of my YouTube notebook.
And then I'm going to put my YouTube notebook on my desk.
And this is the new level of my videos from now on.
Jeez, thanks whoever you are for giving me the idea.
There you go.
And then I'll get down a little bit and there's the level.
So, my car is a great place to make a video.
Plus, I can talk and nobody is going to be around me like, what the fuck is that guy talking about?
Weirdo talking to himself, making a video.
You know, even when I do that in parking lots and supermarkets, people can hear me too.
And they walk by, they're like, oh, God, what a weirdo.
They never, like, look at me, but it makes me feel weird.
You know, sometimes when I got my door open, somebody gets in their car right there.
And I'm like, oh, and then I get, like, distracted.
Yeah, so I'm going to try to from now on not make videos so much in, like, really crowded parking lots, like, in peaceful places like this.
And I can think better that way, you know.
But, yeah, I want to make lots of videos.
Whenever I find places where I can upload videos, I'm like, I'm like, I'm jazzed.
I'm wicked jazzed that there's that library in Bellingham that can upload faster than a motherfucker.
And I have two computers, so I'm going to splice up the videos, half of them on one computer, half of them on the other computer.
And upload them at the same time and then work all day long, like, put in one of those 12-hour days.
And then just all of my videos.
It doesn't matter how many videos I make up here.
I can make 100 hour-long videos, and they're all going to get uploaded when I get back down there.
I fucking love that library.
It's insanely fast on the internet.
Oh, my God.
I love it.
And it's consistent, too.
It's consistently good.
Okay, so that's my videos on making videos.
I do have other kinds of videos, like my video, my exercise videos.
I'm going to do that more later on, hopefully.
I need to be able to drag myself away from my project, dude.
I am sucked into my project like a crackhead is sucked into smoking crack.
I cannot tear myself away from it.
I get on there, and I cannot get up to pee.
I have to pee, and I'm like, oh, and I can do a little more.
Oh, and I feel like hell.
I'm, like, toxic.
And I'm like, oh, God, no.
Keep going.
Oh, you know.
It's fucking bad for my body, dude.
So, you know, one of these days when I, like, my body breaks down, I'm going to be like, okay, fuck this.
I'm going to use that same neurotic tendency to swim down the Mississippi River, and I'll be neurotic as fuck about that.
And I can do that because I bike toured through Europe, and I was all about bike touring, you know.
I only stopped because I, like, my bike kept breaking, and my bike rack kept breaking.
And, of course, I'd be passing through these big cities, and, of course, I'd have to spend a few days there to check those out.
But, yeah, dude, I could totally – and, like, I could row a boat completely around the world.
It would take me a whole year paddling up, rowing every single goddamn day, and that's all I do, and I'd be totally happy with that.
And it's part of the training.
It's not like I was always like that, but I was trained to be like that from the fucking computer program – the computer shit that I do.
All this tedious ass – so when I'm done with this – this timeline is going to turn me into a better person.
It's going to make me a really patient person, and I'll basically be able to do anything.
Nothing is going to daunt me when I'm done with this timeline.
Like, I'm going to have, like, the idea to create a fucking – you know, like, redesign the political system.
You know, but in order to do that, I have to figure out, like, what every single law made was and why it was made, and then how to, like, redo it and then catalog all of them.
And it will be just as time-consuming as my timeline.
I'll be like, okay, whatever.
I don't fucking care.
I've been here and done that before, you know.
And as time goes on, I think I will be less and less neurotic, and I'll be able to tear myself away and do other stuff at the same time, especially if I have money.

You know, maybe – I'm hoping one day I'll be able to make money with my timeline, enough money to supplement my oil income so that I could actually, like, you know, pay for, like, vitamins and keep myself healthy.
And, like, you know, like, go on a vacation, you know, go, like, bike touring and stuff.
But that's how I make videos.
That's what I do.
Now, how do I make – how do I make my travel videos?
That's another kind of videos that I do, travel videos.
I'm really good at travel documentarialist.
When I do my kayak trip around the Caribbean, I'm going to film everything.
I'm going to film videos from my helmet.
I'm going to film videos from a panorama of, like, when I'm in the middle of the sea explaining everything.
And if you see my YouTube, bike touring videos are like that.
You know, I'm like, I'm here.
There's the city.
This is the direction I'm going.
There's the city.
I'm going to zoom in on the city.
There's the sign that says that city is 20 miles away.
You know, there's the mountain that I just went over.
There's the next mountain I'm going to go over.
And, you know, this is how many miles I went today.
This is how many miles it is from here to here.
That's my camp.
This is how I find a camp.
And I would love it if, like, adventure people who did that did that kind of shit.
You know, like, there is this couple who, there's this dude who rode his bike all the way around the world.
And then he rode a boat all the way across the Atlantic with his girlfriend.
And she was actually the first woman to do that.
This was, like, 10 years ago or something.
I don't know.
But they were in a hurricane for, like, four days.
Like, a totally hardcore hurricane.
And she wrote a book about it.
And I want to read the book.
But that would have been awesome if they had actually filmed it.
And they did film a little bit of it.
They filmed themselves on the, with their little helmets that they made out of bras.
Just in case they get hit.
They don't get themselves knocked out.
But I wanted to see them filming outside of the window and actually looking at the waves.
Because, I mean, didn't they say the waves are, like, 40 feet?
Like, I want to see a 40-foot wave.
I want to see what it looks like, you know.
Because then I'd feel like I'm out there.
Like, what about all these people who sail boats around the world?
And they're like, oh, it was in the 30-foot waves.
And it's like, it's like, yeah, you got a nice-looking website.
You've got, you know, you're getting all kinds of attention.
You've got your blogs.
Where the hell is the video of the 40-foot wave?
I mean, how hard could that be?
I want to see that shit.
Anyways, that's what I'm all about.
So, like, when I swim down the Mississippi, I'm going to be filming.
I'm going to be doing the flogging the whole time down.
I'm going to be filming every single stretch of it.
When I do my kayak trip, I'm going to film, like, I'm going to film, like, every time I see land, I'm going to film it.
I'm going to go, okay, there's the land.
My GPS is that far out.
It's 3 o'clock.
And then when I get there, I'll be like, okay, now it's 8 o'clock.
It took me four hours to get this many miles.
The wind was going this direction.
And, you know, you totally document the fuck out of it.
And that's why I made such a big kayak trip website.
It's to be the skeleton to all the information when I go there so that when people want to do it, because people will want to do it after that,
They'll have all the information they need.
And they'll have all the videos and everything.
There's not too much ways to document.
There's another kind of videos.
I like to do, I like interviews.
I'd love to do interviews with people, but nobody wants to be interviewed.
I've, you know, asked people in the streets, like, I asked some lady who's going to climb El Cap, and she's like, no way.
And she had talked to me for a little bit, but I couldn't interview her.
You know, nobody in my family wants to get interviewed.
None of my friends would put themselves on the internet.
It's, like, weird.
But I would, one of these days, once people, like, become not so freaked out about, not so superstitious, I'd like to interview people.
And, like, especially my brother.
I want to interview my brother about Buddhism, because he's a Buddhist.
He's, like, a hardcore Buddhist.
And he's, like, against my video.
He thinks I'm an idiot about my, where Buddhism went wrong.
He's like, I couldn't even watch it, because you're just so ignorant.
You don't know anything about Buddhism.
And I was like, what do I not know?
We're done with this conversation.
And I'm like, oh, well, let's have a conversation on YouTube.
And then we can actually, you can tell me why you think I don't know anything about Buddhism, even though you never even watched the video.
He's like, no, I don't do that.
I'm not going to put my face up on the internet at all.
You know, and it's like, one of these days, that's kind of one thing I want to do.
Like, maybe if he's really old, about to die.
Come on, let's do that video.
What?
Oh, no.
I don't know, whatever.
But, yeah, and then my exercise videos.
I love the exercise videos, because it's PRs, and about all the different exercises.
And I love having, I'd love to know how many of, like, I would fucking love, and I used to email a bunch of bodybuilder chicks, but they never emailed me back, so I gave up on it.
But I'd love to know how many, like, dumbbell squat cleans somebody can do.
You know, not just, like, how many, how long it takes them to do, like, Fran, like, whatever, like, where you pull the 135-pound barbell up.
Because I don't, I can't, number one, I'm not allowed to film in a gym.
None of the 24-hour fitnesses and none of the goals gyms let me film in there.
And so I can't do it.
I can only do it with dumbbells.
That's the only thing that I have, 35-pound dumbbells.
So it'd be awesome if I could type 35-pound dumbbell, or if I could write some muscle girl who, and those muscle chicks are always allowed to film in the gyms.
Like, yeah, fucking how many, film yourself, do, show me how many dumbbell squat cleans you can do with 35-pound dumbbells.
And then if I saw that video, I've got my 35ers right here, I'd get out there and I'd be like, cool, oh, fuck.
She beat me.
And then that'd be motivation to do more.
So that's, that's the whole idea behind my Kyle's Gym videos.
I love it.
Yeah, I just like, I like comparing myself with other people.
Like, there's this one girl who, I don't have perfect push-ups with me.
And I do have them at home and I should have brought them with me.
When I go to Boulder, I'm going to grab them.
But she did 50 perfect push-up push-ups.
Woo!
I can't do that many.
So that's my goal, to do 50 perfect push-up push-ups.
And of course, film myself every time, you know.
And if like, and if somebody's in my league, like I could say I could only do 30, and if somebody else can only do 31, then they're my competition.
I can watch them.
So that's the idea behind those.
What other videos do I have?
Uh, well, screen captures on my website.
All the ideas on my website.
Screen captures are awesome.
And that's really easy to do.
I just bought a program called CaptureWiz.
And I'm sure there's a million programs.
And even better, one's better than that these days.
But, yeah.
I just do CaptureWiz.
Go over, push record.
And then once it's done, I transfer it to, um, with Windows Media Player to a file that, uh, YouTube can take, which makes them a lot smaller.
And, uh, I plug in my microphone to the computer so that the audio is really good.
And there you go.
I mean, other people should do that, too.
Like, if you have a website, you should do, you should do a tour of your website.
Hey, look at my website.
Do this.
Do, watch.
Follow me in here.
And then I could be at my computer and, like, watching and, like, following.
Oh, cool.
Um, yeah.
Here comes a Volkswagen van.
So that's how I do videos.
Um, it's basically how much, I do as many videos as I can do uploading stuff.
If I'm around a place where I can upload shit, I'm starting to do, I want to do my, all my videos in Spanish, too, pretty soon.
Anyways, that's me.
That's enough.
That's long enough for that video.
